name: vscode-abap-remote-fs
description: VS Code-based Agentic AI Platform for SAP ABAP Development

scripts.vscode:prepublish: cd client && cd .. && npm run webpack
scripts.webpack: npm run build_modules&&cd server && npm run webpack && cd ../client && npm run webpack
scripts.build_modules: cd modules/abapObject&& npm run build&&cd ../abapfs&& npm run build&&cd ../sharedapi&& npm run build
scripts.test: npm run test-object && npm run test-abapfs && npm run test-server && npm run test-client
scripts.inst_dep_sharedapi: cd modules/sharedapi&& npm i && npm run build&& cd ../..
scripts.inst_dep_object: cd modules/abapObject&& npm i && npm run build&& cd ../..
scripts.inst_dep_abapfs: cd modules/abapfs&& npm i && npm run build&& cd ../..
scripts.release:vsix: npm run build && npm run createpackage
scripts.clean: rimraf out *.vsix node_modules modules/sharedapi/out modules/sharedapi/node_modules&& cd server && npm run clean&& cd ../client &&npm run clean && cd ..
scripts.postinstall: npm run inst_dep_sharedapi && npm run inst_dep_object &&npm run inst_dep_abapfs && cd server && npm i && cd ../client && npm i

connId.description: Absolute path to a text file.

debugUser.description: User to debug for (defaults to current user)

viewsWelcome.contents:
Load S/4HANA readiness data to see which custom objects reference removed or changed SAP objects.
[Load Dashboard](command:abapfs.s4hLoad)

languageModelTools.name: search_abap_objects
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search ABAP objects by name pattern. Wildcards: * ?. Custom code: prefix Z* or Y* (Z*ARTICLE*, not *ARTICLE*). Standard SAP: BAPI_*, CL_*, /SAP/*. MANDATORY before code generation: training data outdated — ALWAYS verify objects exist first, read signatures with get_abap_object_lines, then generate. Unverified code WILL fail at runtime.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search for ABAP objects in the SAP system

pattern.description: Search pattern for object names. Supports wildcards (* and ?). START WITH Z* FOR CUSTOM OBJECTS (Z*invoice*, not *invoice*) Examples: 'USER', '*CUSTOMER*', 'BAPI_*',Z*ART*

types.description: Object types to search for.Must provide type or list of types.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of results to return

languageModelTools.name: get_abap_object_lines
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read active ABAP source. objectType disambiguates same-named objects (MATNR exists as DTEL and DOMA). methodName extracts one method body from a class. MANDATORY before code generation: training data has wrong params — ALWAYS read exact signatures before calling functions/methods, never guess. Unverified params WILL fail at runtime. IMPORTANT: shows committed code only, not unsaved local edits — use VS Code file tools for fresh edits.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get source code lines from ABAP objects

objectName.description: Name of the ABAP object to retrieve lines from

objectType.description: Optional: Specific object type to avoid ambiguity.

methodName.description: CLASSES ONLY: extract one method body, METHOD xxx. → ENDMETHOD. Examples: 'FACTORY', 'CONSTRUCTOR', 'IF_INTERFACE~METHOD_NAME'. Overrides startLine/lineCount.

startLine.description: Starting line number (1-based, like VS Code). Use 1 for first line, 2 for second line, etc.

languageModelTools.name: search_abap_object_lines
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search text inside ABAP source. Literal or regex (isRegexp=true). Prefer over VS Code grep — ABAP source lives on remote system, grep_search misses it. objectName wildcards scan multiple objects (max 10). Class structure trick: regex '^\s*(CLASS-)?METHODS?\s+\w+' with contextLines=0 lists all method names. IMPORTANT: searches committed code only, not unsaved local edits.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search for text within ABAP object source code

objectName.description: Name of the ABAP object to search within. Supports wildcards (*) to search multiple objects - use maxObjects to control how many

searchTerm.description: Text or regex pattern to find in source. Literal for simple search; regex for advanced matching (e.g., \bICT\b word boundary, METHOD.*restrict pattern, [A-Z]+ char class).

contextLines.description: Number of context lines to include before and after each match

isRegexp.description: Regex mode. True: searchTerm treated as regex — word boundaries (\bword\b), patterns (METHOD.*restrict), char classes ([A-Z]+). False: fast literal text match.

maxObjects.description: Max objects to scan when objectName has wildcards (Z*, BAPI_*). 1 = single object, max 10 = multi-object batch. Only applies with wildcards.

languageModelTools.name: get_abap_object_info
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get ABAP object metadata: type, total lines, cache status. Use before retrieving content to understand what kind of object you're dealing with.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get metadata information about ABAP objects

objectName.description: Name of the ABAP object to get information about

objectType.description: Narrow search by type to resolve ambiguity (e.g., MATNR exists as both DTEL and DOMA). FUNC=Function Module, CLAS=Class, TABL=Table, PROG=Report, INTF=Interface, DTEL=Data Element, DDLS=CDS View, DOMA=Domain, TTYP=Table Type, ENQU=Lock Object, MSAG=Message Class, FUGR=Function Group, DEVC=Package, TRAN=Transaction, VIEW=View, SICF=ICF Service, WDYN=Web Dynpro, SPRX=Proxy, XSLT=XSLT, TRANSFORMATIONS=Simple Transformation, SUSH=Auth Object, SUSC=Auth Object Class, PINF=Package Interface, ENHC=Enhancement Impl, ENHS=Enhancement Spot, BADI=BAdI Def, BADII=BAdI Impl, SAMC=AMC Class, SAPC=APC Class, SFSW=Switch Framework, SFBF=Business Function, SFBS=Business Function Set, JOBD=Job Definition, NROB=Number Range, ENHO=Enhancement Impl, SUSO=Auth Object Set, BDEF=Behavior Def, SRVB=Service Binding

languageModelTools.name: get_batch_lines
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read lines from multiple ABAP objects in one call. IMPORTANT: returns committed code only, not unsaved local edits — use VS Code file tools to see fresh edits.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get source code lines from multiple ABAP objects at once

requests.description: Array of requests, each specifying an object and line range

languageModelTools.name: get_object_by_uri
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read ABAP object by direct ADT URI. Use when URI already known from search results — skips name resolution.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Direct access to ABAP objects via URI paths

uri.description: The ADT URI path of the ABAP object (e.g., '/sap/bc/adt/programs/programs/ztest')

languageModelTools.name: create_object_programmatically
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Create new ABAP objects (classes, reports, function groups, function modules, interfaces, data elements, tables, CDS views, packages, service bindings, etc.). Function modules: objectType='FUGR/FF' and ALWAYS provide parentName with the parent function group name. Service bindings: MANDATORY serviceDefinition, bindingType, bindingCategory. Packages: MANDATORY softwareComponent, packageType, transportLayer.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Create new ABAP objects programmatically

objectType.description: Object type, format TYPE/SUBTYPE. PROG/P (report), CLAS/OC (class), FUGR/F (function group), FUGR/FF (function module — requires parentName=parent function group), INTF/OI (interface), DTEL/DE (data element), TABL/DT (table), DDLS/DF (CDS view), DOMA/DD (domain), MSAG/N (message class), DEVC/K (package), SRVD/SRV (service definition), SRVB/SVB (service binding), BDEF/BDO (behavior definition), and others. All SAP creatable types supported.

name.description: Object name (e.g., 'ZTEST_REPORT'). For database tables (TABL/DT), name must be 14 characters or less for logical database compatibility. For other objects, ensure you don't exceed allowed object length limits

description.description: Object description (max 60 characters – shorten or truncate if longer; SAP short text limit)

parentName.description: Parent object name. Required for function modules ('FUGR/FF'): pass the parent function group name, not the function module name. Optional for other object types such as includes/methods.

additionalOptions.description: Type-specific options. MANDATORY for service bindings (SRVB/SVB): serviceDefinition, bindingType, bindingCategory. MANDATORY for packages (DEVC/K): softwareComponent, packageType, transportLayer.

serviceDefinition.description: MANDATORY for service bindings (SRVB/SVB): The name of an existing service definition (SRVD/SRV) to bind to (e.g., 'ZSD_MY_SERVICE')

bindingType.description: MANDATORY for service bindings (SRVB/SVB): Binding protocol type. Currently only 'ODATA' is supported

bindingCategory.description: MANDATORY for service bindings (SRVB/SVB): '0' = OData V2 Web API, '1' = OData V2 UI

type.description: Type of transport request: 'new' to create a new one, 'existing' to use an existing one

description.description: Description for new transport request (required for type 'new')

languageModelTools.name: get_abap_object_url
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Return SAP GUI WebGUI URL for an ABAP object (SE38 reports, SE24 classes, SE37 function modules). Use for browser automation — not for opening in editor.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Generate SAP GUI URL for ABAP objects for browser automation

languageModelTools.name: get_abap_object_workspace_uri
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get adt:// workspace URI for an ABAP object — usable with standard VS Code file ops (replace_string_in_file etc.) and/or replace_string_in_abap_object if available. Use when modifying/editing objects in editor OR when user asks to open file in editor. All params MANDATORY for exact object identification. NOTE: for function modules, pass the parent function group name with 'FUGR/F' type, NOT 'FUGR/FF'.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get workspace URI for ABAP objects for direct file access

objectName.description: Exact name of the ABAP object to open (e.g., 'ZXX_ARTICLE_REPORT', 'ZCL_MY_CLASS'). Must be exact match, no wildcards.

objectType.description: Exact object type — required to avoid opening wrong object. Examples: 'PROG/P' (report), 'CLAS/OC' (class), 'FUGR/F' (function group), 'INTF/OI' (interface). For function modules: pass parent function group name with 'FUGR/F', NOT 'FUGR/FF'. Use full subtype (ENHO/XHH correct vs ENHO may fail).

languageModelTools.name: create_mermaid_diagram
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Render Mermaid diagram in interactive webview with zoom controls.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Create professional diagrams in interactive webview with zoom controls

code.description: The Mermaid diagram code to render

diagramType.description: The type of diagram to create (auto-detect if not specified)

languageModelTools.name: validate_mermaid_syntax
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Validate Mermaid syntax without rendering.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Validate diagram syntax before rendering

code.description: The Mermaid diagram code to validate

suppressErrors.description: Return false instead of throwing errors for invalid syntax

languageModelTools.name: get_mermaid_documentation
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get Mermaid syntax docs and examples per diagram type.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get syntax documentation and examples

diagramType.description: Specific diagram type to get documentation for (all for complete documentation)

includeExamples.description: Include syntax examples in the documentation

languageModelTools.name: detect_mermaid_diagram_type
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Auto-detect Mermaid diagram type from code.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Auto-detect diagram type from code

code.description: The Mermaid diagram code to analyze

languageModelTools.name: create_test_documentation
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Build Word doc from Playwright test screenshots grouped by scenario.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Generate Word test documentation from screenshots

scenarioName.description: Name of the test scenario (e.g., 'Happy Path Login', 'Error Handling')

scenarioDescription.description: Detailed description of what this scenario tests

filePath.description: Full file path to the screenshot (e.g., 'C:\Users\...\screenshot.png')

description.description: Description of what the screenshot shows

screenshots.description: Array of screenshots for this scenario

scenarios.description: Array of test scenarios with their screenshots

reportTitle.description: Title for the test documentation report (optional, defaults to 'Test Documentation Report')

testDate.description: Date of testing in DD-MM-YYYY format (optional, defaults to current date)

languageModelTools.name: execute_data_query
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run ABAP SQL or display structured data in interactive webview. ALWAYS call get_abap_sql_syntax FIRST — ABAP SQL differs from standard SQL, skipping causes syntax errors. Supports sort, filter, row range. For internal mode, rowRange is MANDATORY (max 1000 rows). Default displayMode=ui. Always set meaningful title. Non-SAP data (JIRA, tasks) via 'data' param instead of 'sql'.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Execute SQL queries, display structured data, and visualize in interactive tables

sql.description: The SQL query to execute (e.g., 'SELECT * FROM mara WHERE matnr LIKE 'MAT%''). OPTIONAL if webviewId or data provided. MANDATORY if no webviewId and no data (new SQL query)

items.description: Row data as key-value pairs matching column names

data.description: Direct data input instead of SQL query (mutually exclusive with sql). Use this to display JIRA issues, task lists, or any structured data

displayMode.description: How to handle results: 'internal' returns data to you for analysis (rowRange mandatory), 'ui' displays in interactive webview for user

webviewId.description: Optional: ID of existing webview to manipulate. If provided, you can sort/filter existing data without re-querying SAP. If not provided, creates new webview with new SQL query

title.description: Webview title. Use meaningful descriptions: 'All Articles from MARA', 'Articles Created by John.Doe', 'JIRA Issues - High Priority'. Helps users understand the data. Updates existing webviews when provided.

maxRows.description: Safety cap on rows AFTER SQL execution (default 1000). NOT added to your SQL — applied post-query to prevent system overload. NOTE: ABAP SQL 'UP TO x ROWS' clauses don't work via ADT — use maxRows instead to limit fetch.

start.description: Starting row index (0-based). Applied AFTER sorting and filtering

end.description: Ending row index (exclusive). Applied AFTER sorting and filtering. Range size (end-start) cannot exceed 1000 rows for internal mode safety.

rowRange.description: Row range to extract AFTER all processing. MANDATORY for internal mode — prevents accidental large data transfers. Range size max 1000 rows. Example: {start:0, end:10} = first 10 rows after sort/filter.

column.description: Column name to sort by (case-sensitive, use exact SAP field names)

direction.description: Sort direction: 'asc' for ascending, 'desc' for descending

sortColumns.description: Multi-column sort in sequence (first = highest priority). Example: [{'column':'MATNR','direction':'asc'},{'column':'WERKS','direction':'desc'}] sorts by MATNR asc, then WERKS desc for ties.

filters.description: Optional: Apply filters to results. Supports wildcards: 'MAT*' matches anything starting with MAT, '*ABC*' contains ABC

resetSorting.description: Optional: Clear all existing sorting before applying new sorts. Use when you want to start fresh

resetFilters.description: Optional: Clear all existing filters before applying new filters. Use when you want to start fresh

languageModelTools.name: get_abap_sql_syntax
languageModelTools.modelDescription: MANDATORY before execute_data_query: ABAP SQL syntax guide — differences from standard SQL, reserved keywords, field naming, table format. MUST call first to prevent syntax errors. No params.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get ABAP SQL syntax documentation before writing queries

languageModelTools.name: run_atc_analysis
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run ABAP Test Cockpit checks or fetch finding documentation. Actions: 'run_analysis' returns findings with check variant + docUri per finding (variant name explains context — e.g., S4HANA_READINESS = upgrade compat, not code quality). 'get_documentation' fetches full doc — often contains critical detail missing from finding title (e.g., table name RSEG). Always fetch docs before suggesting fixes. Each finding has unique docs even when titles repeat.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run ABAP Test Cockpit analysis with AI integration

action.description: 'run_analysis' (default): run ATC checks. 'get_documentation': fetch detailed check doc — use docUri from prior run_analysis result to identify the finding.

objectName.description: Specific ABAP object name to analyze (e.g., 'ZDUMMY1', 'ZCL_MY_CLASS')

objectType.description: Object type (e.g., 'PROG/P' for reports, 'CLAS/OC' for classes). Used with objectName for precise targeting

objectUri.description: Direct ADT URI path (e.g., 'adt://dev100/sap/bc/adt/programs/programs/zdummy1'). Takes precedence over objectName if provided

useActiveFile.description: Use currently active ABAP file if no object provided. Set true ONLY if file open in editor and explicit object spec not required. Always prefer objectName or URI — active editor is last resort.

scope.description: Analysis scope (future enhancement) - currently only 'object' is supported

docUri.description: For get_documentation action: the docUri of the finding (exact value from run_analysis results). Each finding includes a docUri field containing the documentation URL.

languageModelTools.name: get_atc_decorations
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read current ATC highlights visible in editor (errors/warnings/info). Companion to run_atc_analysis.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Access current ATC visual decorations in editor

fileUri.description: Optional specific file URI to get decorations for. If not provided, returns decorations for all files with ATC highlights

languageModelTools.name: manage_text_elements
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read, create, or update text elements (translatable strings) in programs, classes, function groups.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Manage text elements in ABAP programs, classes, and function groups

objectName.description: Name of the ABAP object to manage text elements for (e.g., 'ZPROGRAM', 'ZCL_CLASS', 'ZFG_GROUP')

objectType.description: Type of ABAP object: 'PROGRAM' for reports/programs, 'CLASS' for classes, 'FUNCTION_GROUP' for function groups

action.description: Action to perform: 'read' to get existing text elements, 'create' to add new ones, 'update' to modify existing ones

textElements.description: Array of text elements (required for create/update actions)

maxLength.description: Optional maximum length constraint for the text

connectionId.description: SAP connection ID (uses active if omitted)

languageModelTools.name: open_object
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Open ABAP object in VS Code editor by name. Use when user wants to view or edit the object.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Open ABAP objects in editor by name

objectName.description: Name of the ABAP object to open (e.g., 'ZCL_MY_CLASS', 'BAPI_USER_GET_DETAIL')

languageModelTools.name: run_unit_tests
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run ABAP unit tests for an object. Results shown in VS Code Testing panel with pass/fail.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run unit tests for ABAP objects

objectName.description: Name of the ABAP object to test (e.g., 'ZCL_MY_CLASS')

languageModelTools.name: create_test_include
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Create test include (unit test class) for an existing ABAP class. Auto-opens in editor.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Create unit test class for ABAP classes

className.description: Name of the ABAP class to create test include for (e.g., 'ZCL_MY_CLASS')

languageModelTools.name: manage_transport_requests
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Manage SAP transport requests. Actions: get_user_transports, get_transport_details, get_transport_objects, compare_transports. IMPORTANT workaround for older SAP systems: on compatibility error, MUST query E070/E071/E071K directly via execute_data_query.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Manage SAP transport requests - get details, objects, and compare transports

transportNumber.description: Transport number for get_transport_details and get_transport_objects actions

transportNumbers.description: Array of transport numbers for compare_transports action (minimum 2 required)

user.description: Username for get_user_transports action (optional, defaults to current user)

languageModelTools.name: abap_debug_session
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Start/stop/check ABAP debug session. action: start | stop | status.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Manage ABAP debugging sessions

languageModelTools.name: abap_debug_breakpoint
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Set or remove ABAP breakpoints. filePath must be full adt:// workspace URI — get it via get_abap_object_workspace_uri first. Supports conditional breakpoints.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Manage breakpoints in ABAP code

filePath.description: Full workspace URI for the ABAP source file. MUST use format: adt://connectionId/path/to/file.prog.abap. Get this URI using get_abap_object_workspace_uri tool.

languageModelTools.name: abap_debug_step
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Step ABAP debug execution. stepType: continue (F5), stepOver (F6), stepInto (F7), stepReturn (F8), jumpToLine (needs targetLine).
languageModelTools.userDescription: Control debugging execution flow

stepType.description: Type of debug step to execute

languageModelTools.name: abap_debug_variable
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Inspect ABAP variables during debug. Call abap_debug_stack first to get frameId (large numbers like 1000000000000, not 0). filter narrows table contents. filterPattern matches variable names (e.g., 'LT_*'). expandStructures / expandTables for inline expansion. maxVariables caps scope listing.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Inspect variables and evaluate expressions during debugging

threadId.description: Debug thread ID (usually 1 for single-threaded debugging)

frameId.description: Stack frame ID from abap_debug_stack tool. REQUIRED - get this value by calling abap_debug_stack first. Frame IDs are large numbers like 1000000000000.

rowStart.description: Start row for internal table data (0-based)

rowCount.description: Number of rows to return from table

filter.description: Filter condition for internal table content

maxVariables.description: Maximum variables to show in scope listings

filterPattern.description: Pattern to filter variable names (e.g., 'LT_*' for all tables, 'LS_*' for structures)

expandTables.description: Show first few table rows inline

languageModelTools.name: abap_debug_stack
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get current ABAP debug call stack (frame IDs, methods, files, lines). frameId values needed for abap_debug_variable.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Display the current call stack

languageModelTools.name: abap_debug_status
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Check ABAP debug session status — active sessions, threads, execution state.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Check debugging session status

languageModelTools.name: analyze_abap_dumps
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Analyze ABAP runtime dumps (ST22). Actions: list_dumps (ID, error type, timestamp), analyze_dump (needs dumpId — structured detail for root-cause analysis).
languageModelTools.userDescription: Analyze ABAP runtime dumps and errors

action.description: Action to perform: list_dumps (show available dumps) or analyze_dump (analyze specific dump)

dumpId.description: Dump ID for analyze_dump action (required for analyze_dump)

maxResults.description: Maximum number of dumps to return for list_dumps (default: 20, max: 100)

includeFullContent.description: Include full HTML dump content for AI analysis (for analyze_dump action)

languageModelTools.name: analyze_abap_traces
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Analyze ABAP performance traces. Actions: list_runs, list_configurations, analyze_run, get_statements (auto-switches to hitlist for aggregated traces), get_hitlist. Identifies DB bottlenecks and ABAP hotspots. traceId must be full URL-encoded path.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Analyze ABAP performance traces for optimization

action.description: Action to perform: list_runs, list_configurations, analyze_run, get_statements (auto-switches to hitlist if trace is aggregated), or get_hitlist

traceId.description: Full trace ID path (required for analyze_run, get_statements, get_hitlist). MUST be complete path with URL encoding preserved (e.g., '/sap/bc/adt/runtime/traces/abaptraces/sapkada01_KAD_52%2cAT520019'). DO NOT use partial paths, unencoded commas, or just the trace name.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of results for listing actions (default: 20)

includeDetails.description: Include detailed performance data where applicable

languageModelTools.name: find_where_used
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Where-used analysis for ABAP objects, methods, variables, symbols. Large result sets (1000+): use startIndex for pagination, filter.objectNamePattern (e.g., 'Z*') or filter.excludeSystemObjects=true to find custom Z/Y code without dumping thousands. WARNING: includeSnippets slow above 50 results — set only when really needed.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Find where ABAP objects and symbols are used

objectName.description: Name of the ABAP object to find references for

objectType.description: Optional: Object type to avoid ambiguity (e.g., 'CLAS/OC', 'PROG/P')

searchTerm.description: Optional: Specific symbol/method/variable to search for within the object

line.description: Optional: Specific line number for context-sensitive search (1-based)

character.description: Optional: Character position for precise symbol search (0-based)

maxResults.description: Maximum number of references to return (default: 50)

includeSnippets.description: Include code snippets showing usage context. WARNING: Requesting snippets for >50 results can be slow. Default: false. Set to true only when really needed,especially for large result sets.

startIndex.description: Result offset (0-based). Use for pagination to skip earlier results and access later ones — essential for navigating large result sets. Example: startIndex=5000 starts at position 5000.

filter.description: Filter criteria to narrow down results. Use to find specific objects without retrieving thousands of results. Especially useful for finding custom Z/Y objects in large result sets.

objectNamePattern.description: Filter by object name pattern with wildcards. Examples: 'Z*' (starts with Z), '*CUSTOM*' (contains CUSTOM), 'ZMD_*' (starts with ZMD_). Use to find custom objects efficiently.

objectTypes.description: Filter by specific object types. Examples: ['PROG/P'] (only programs), ['CLAS/OC', 'INTF/OI'] (classes and interfaces)

excludeSystemObjects.description: Exclude SAP standard objects (objects not starting with Z or Y). Very useful for finding only custom code in large result sets. Default: false

languageModelTools.name: get_sap_system_info
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get SAP system info: client (T000), system type (S/4HANA vs ECC), release (SVERS), timezone (TTZCU/TTZZ with UTC offset + DST). includeComponents=true adds CVERS software list. System type and timezone always returned.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get SAP system information (current client, system type, timezone, components, release)

connectionId.description: SAP connection ID (e.g., 'dev100'). Required parameter to identify which SAP system to query.

includeComponents.description: Whether to include the full list of software components (from CVERS). Default is false to reduce response size. System type (S/4HANA vs ECC) and timezone are always included regardless of this setting.

languageModelTools.name: get_connected_systems
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List SAP connection IDs currently connected in VS Code. Call first if connectionId unknown. No params.
languageModelTools.userDescription: List all connected SAP systems

languageModelTools.name: get_version_history
languageModelTools.modelDescription: ABAP object version history. Actions: list_versions (dates, authors, transports), get_version_source (code at version N), compare_versions (diff between two). Version 1 = most recent.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get version history, retrieve historical code, or compare versions

objectName.description: Name of the ABAP object to get version history for

action.description: Action to perform: list_versions (show history), get_version_source (get code at version), compare_versions (diff two versions)

versionNumber.description: For get_version_source: which version to retrieve (1 = most recent, 2 = second most recent, etc.)

version1.description: For compare_versions: first version number (1 = most recent)

version2.description: For compare_versions: second version number to compare against

maxVersions.description: Maximum versions to show in list_versions (default 20)

languageModelTools.name: manage_subagents
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Configure cost-optimized ABAP subagents. Safe actions: get_status, list_models, list_agents, list_tools, validate. Need user approval: enable, disable, configure, regenerate. Model tier rules — Tier 1 (discoverer, reader, creator, visualizer, documenter): cheapest only (Haiku, Flash, GPT-4o). Tier 2 (usage-analyzer, quality-checker, historian, debugger, troubleshooter, data-analyst): mid (GPT-4o, Sonnet 4, Gemini 2.5 Pro). Tier 3 (orchestrator, code-reviewer): premium allowed with explicit consent. STOP and ask user before Opus / Max / GPT-5.x on any tier — explain cost. Guide new users: explain tier system and cost implications before configuring; use list_agents / list_models / list_tools to show options.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Configure AI subagents for cost-optimized ABAP development (requires explicit user consent)

action.description: Action to perform: enable/disable subagents, get_status, list_models, list_agents, list_tools, configure models, validate configuration, regenerate files

model.description: Model ID to use for this agent

configurations.description: For 'configure' action: array of {agentId, model} pairs to set

languageModelTools.name: abap_fs_documentation
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read/search ABAP FS extension docs (DOCUMENTATION.md) and settings reference (ABAP-FS-SETTINGS.md). Brand ambassador for this extension — 40+ tools, many users don't know exist. Proactively suggest relevant features from context; educate users on capabilities; help them find the right settings. Be enthusiastic but honest, no exaggeration. Always verify docs first before explaining a feature or setting — wrong info kills trust. Setting scope critical: application=user/global, resource=workspace — wrong scope fails silently. Can change Global/Workspace settings yourself but never without explicit user consent. Only reference official repo https://github.com/marcellourbani/vscode_abap_remote_fs — no invented links.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Access ABAP FS extension documentation and settings reference

action.description: get_documentation (read DOCUMENTATION.md), search_documentation (search DOCUMENTATION.md), get_settings (read ABAP-FS-SETTINGS.md), search_settings (search ABAP-FS-SETTINGS.md).

searchQuery.description: Required for search_* actions. Case-insensitive search term to find in the documentation or settings files

startLine.description: For get_* actions: Starting line number (1-based). Use 1 for first line

lineCount.description: For get_* actions: Number of lines to retrieve. For search_* actions: context lines around each match

languageModelTools.name: manage_heartbeat
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Background SAP monitor + reminder service. Call action=status first — verifies service running and HB model configured. Actions: status, start, stop, trigger, history, add_task, remove_task, update_task, enable_task, disable_task, list_tasks, get_watchlist. TIMEZONE RULE: for ANY monitoring task, get SAP timezone via get_sap_system_info first — HB model uses it when interpreting dates/times from SAP. Scheduled reminders ('tomorrow at 10am'): set startAt as ISO timestamp converted to UTC per SAP timezone. reminderOnly=true notifies once then auto-removes. Dump monitoring: checkInstructions must reference analyze_abap_dumps tool, not raw SQL. sampleQuery = direct SQL for HB model (e.g., E070 transport monitoring). cooldownMinutes prevents re-notify spam. removeWhenDone auto-cleans on condition met.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Personal assistant for reminders and background SAP monitoring

reason.description: For 'trigger': reason for manual heartbeat. For 'add_task': why this task was added (context for HB model)

count.description: For 'history': number of entries to show

connectionId.description: For 'add_task': SAP connection ID for this task

removeWhenDone.description: For 'add_task': auto-remove task when condition is met

sampleQuery.description: For 'add_task': Pre-built SQL query for HB model to execute directly (e.g., SELECT FROM E070 WHERE...)

checkInstructions.description: For 'add_task': Step-by-step instructions for HB model to follow

priority.description: For 'add_task': Task priority (high=immediate alert, low=can batch)

alertThreshold.description: For 'add_task': Only alert if count exceeds this threshold

cooldownMinutes.description: For 'add_task': Don't re-notify within this many minutes

expiresAt.description: For 'add_task': Auto-remove task after this ISO timestamp

maxChecks.description: For 'add_task': Auto-remove after this many checks

startAt.description: For 'add_task': Don't check until this ISO timestamp (for scheduled reminders like 'remind me tomorrow at 10am')

reminderOnly.description: For 'add_task': Simple reminder - just notify once and auto-remove

result.description: For 'update_task': result of the check to record in lastResult

lastNotifiedAt.description: For 'update_task': ISO timestamp when user was last notified

lastNotifiedFindings.description: For 'update_task': Summary of what was included in last notification (IDs, counts)

modifiedBy.description: For 'add_task', 'update_task': who is making the change

languageModelTools.name: adt_discovery_export
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Export full ADT discovery tree (workspaces, collections, RES_APP classes from SEOMETAREL) to markdown files. Pair with adt-api-discovery skill to investigate ADT REST endpoints — handler classes, Simple Transformations, request/response schemas.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Export ADT API discovery data to markdown files for AI-assisted endpoint investigation

languageModelTools.name: abap_activate
languageModelTools.displayName: Activate ABAP objects - equivalent of compiling
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Activate ABAP object at given URL (equivalent to compiling). Returns activated objects + any activation errors.

url.description: Url of the object to activate. Usually the current file's URL.

commands.title: Mark All Feed Entries as Read
ABAP Compare With another SAP System
SAP Data Workbook: Set Cell Row Limit
Activate current object (will also save if dirty)
Clean ABAP Code with ABAP Cleaner
Pull from current branch (overwrites all changes)
Open SAP GUI in embedded WebView
Open SAP GUI in external web browser
List transports for a given user
Generate RAP UI Service from Table...

keybindings.when: editorTextFocus && resourceScheme == adt && editorLangId == abap && abapfs.cleanerAvailable

view/item/context.when: view == abapfs.abapgit && viewItem == repository || view == abapfs.abapgit && viewItem == repository_push
view == abapfs.abapgit && viewItem == repository || view == abapfs.abapgit && viewItem == repository_push
view == abapfs.abapgit && viewItem == repository || view == abapfs.abapgit && viewItem == repository_push
view == abapfs.atcFinds && viewItem == finding || view == abapfs.atcFinds && viewItem == finding_exempted
view == abapfs.traces && ( viewItem == system || viewItem == configfolder || viewItem == runfolder )
view == abapfs.traces && ( viewItem == run || viewItem == configuration )

url.description: Server base URL. the http(s) URL of your SAP dev server

authMethod.description: Authentication method for this SAP system
markdownEnumDescriptions: Browser SSO (SAML/Kerberos via browser cookie capture)
OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code + PKCE (on-premise SAP via SOAUTH2)

certAuth.description: X.509 client certificate configuration (when authMethod is 'cert')

certPath.description: Path to client certificate PEM file

keyPath.description: Path to private key PEM file (not required for PKCS#12 .p12/.pfx files)

caPath.description: Path to CA certificate for SAP server verification

kerberosAuth.description: Kerberos/SPNEGO configuration (when authMethod is 'kerberos'). All fields are optional — authentication uses Windows SSPI via UseDefaultCredentials automatically. These fields are reserved for future SPN customization.

sapHostname.description: SAP server FQDN (optional, not required for standard SSPI auth)

oauthOnPrem.description: On-premise SAP OAuth 2.0 configuration (when authMethod is 'oauth_onprem'). Requires SOAUTH2 setup on SAP system.

clientId.description: OAuth client ID registered in SAP transaction SOAUTH2

allowSelfSigned.title: Do not validate SSL certificates (lowers the connection security)

maxDebugThreads.description: Maximum number of concurrent debug threads per session

sapGui.description: SapGUI integration (optional, default guessed from URL)

markdownEnumDescriptions: Use Webgui in chrome for executing without exposing passwords

Use Webgui in your browser for executing 
> $(warning) **might expose your password**

Use Webgui embedded in vscode for executing 
> $(warning) **might expose your password**

abapfs.sapGui.useIntegratedBrowser.markdownDescription: Use VS Code's integrated browser instead of the embedded WebView for SAP GUI. Disable this if you prefer Webviews but Webviews can show a blank page (e.g. due to SAP clickjacking frame protection).

abapfs.sapGui.openXmlInGui.markdownDescription: Automatically open XML/metadata objects (like message classes, simple transformations, service bindings) in SAP GUI instead of as XML text files in the editor.

abapfs.copilot.professionalNotifications.description: 😴 I am boring - Keep notifications professional (disable fun emoji prefixes)

abapfs.mcpServer.autoStart.description: Automatically start the MCP server when the extension activates. The MCP server allows external AI clients (Cursor, Claude Desktop) to use ABAP FS tools.

abapfs.mcpServer.port.description: Port number for the MCP server. Change if the default port conflicts with other services.

abapfs.mcpServer.apiKey.description: API key for MCP server authentication. When set, clients must include this key in the Authorization header (Bearer token). Leave empty to allow unauthenticated local access (not recommended for shared machines).

abapfs.autoOpenUnsupportedInGui.description: Automatically open unsupported object types in SAP GUI. When disabled, shows a message with instructions to manually open using Ctrl+Shift+F5.

enabled.description: Enable ABAP Cleaner integration for automatic code formatting

executablePath.description: Full path to abap-cleanerc.exe (command line version). Example: C:\tools\abap-cleaner\abap-cleanerc.exe

profilePath.description: Optional path to custom cleanup profile (.cfj file). Leave empty to use default profile

targetRelease.description: Target ABAP release for cleanup rules (determines which language features to use)

showStatistics.description: Show cleanup statistics after processing (number of changes made)

showAppliedRules.description: Show which cleanup rules were applied (verbose output)

cleanOnSave.description: Automatically clean ABAP code when saving files

lineRange.description: Optional line range specification for partial cleaning

enabled.description: Enable line range cleaning (clean only selected lines)

expandRange.description: Automatically expand range to include complete statements

timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds for cleanup operations (30000ms = 30 seconds)

abapfs.localfs.preferGlobal.description: Store non-abap files in a global folder, shared by all workspaces. Defaults to false, which means files are stored in a hidden folder inside each workspace. Enabling this allows sharing files like AI agent configurations across multiple workspaces

abapfs.subagents.enabled.description: Enable AI subagents for optimized ABAP analysis. When enabled, Copilot delegates specialized tasks to cheaper/faster models, reducing costs significantly.

abapfs.subagents.models.description: Model selection per agent. Keys are agent IDs (e.g., 'abap-discoverer'), values are model IDs. Ask Copilot to configure this.

enumDescriptions: Use the existing ABAP FS blame layout with annotations aligned after the code.
Use a GitLens-inspired fixed blame lane before the code with grouped blame blocks.
abapfs.blame.renderMode.description: Choose how file blame annotations are rendered in the editor. This setting is global and applies across all workspaces.

abapfs.heartbeat.enabled.description: Enable heartbeat: periodic LLM monitoring using heartbeat.json watchlist. Background agent checks tasks at configured intervals.

abapfs.heartbeat.model.description: Language model to use for heartbeat (e.g., 'Claude Sonnet 4', 'GPT-4o'). Leave empty for auto-selection.

abapfs.heartbeat.prompt.description: Custom heartbeat prompt. If empty, uses heartbeat.json watchlist to build prompt.

abapfs.heartbeat.ackMaxChars.description: Max chars allowed after HEARTBEAT_OK before treating as an alert.

abapfs.heartbeat.maxHistory.description: Maximum heartbeat history entries to keep.

abapfs.heartbeat.maxConsecutiveErrors.description: Auto-pause heartbeat after this many consecutive errors.

abapfs.heartbeat.activeHours.description: Restrict heartbeat to active hours (local time). Set start/end in 24h format (e.g., '08:00', '22:00').

abapfs.heartbeat.notifyOnAlert.description: Show VS Code notification when heartbeat finds something that needs attention.

abapfs.heartbeat.notifyOnError.description: Show VS Code notification when heartbeat encounters an error.

abapfs.feedSubscriptions.description: Feed subscriptions and polling configuration for SAP systems. Configure via 'ABAP FS: Configure Feeds' command.

additionalProperties.description: Feed configuration for a specific system

enabled.description: Whether this feed is enabled for polling

query.description: Custom query filter for this feed

useDefaultQuery.description: Use the default query provided by the feed

colors.description: Specifies the background color of the ABAP FS GitLens-style blame annotations.
Specifies the foreground color of the ABAP FS GitLens-style blame annotations.
Specifies the background color of the associated line highlights in ABAP FS blame annotations.
Specifies the overview ruler color of the associated line highlights in ABAP FS blame annotations.
Specifies the background color of the selected-line blame annotation in ABAP FS GitLens mode.
Specifies the foreground color of the selected-line blame annotation in ABAP FS GitLens mode.

walkthroughs.title: Getting Started with ABAP FS
walkthroughs.description: New here? Connect to SAP, find and edit objects, activate code — all from VS Code.

steps.title: Already Familiar? Skip Ahead
steps.description:
Already know the basics? Jump to another walkthrough:

[ABAP FS Views & Tools](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%22murbani.vscode-abap-remote-fs%23abapfs.viewsAndTools%22) — Transports, debugging, ATC, unit tests, dumps, traces, version history.

[AI Tools for ABAP](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%22murbani.vscode-abap-remote-fs%23abapfs.aiTools%22) — Use Copilot or any AI client with your SAP system.

[Advanced & Power Features](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%22murbani.vscode-abap-remote-fs%23abapfs.advanced%22) — MCP server, debug recording, subagents, heartbeat.

Reopen anytime: **Help** menu → **Welcome**.

steps.title: Set Up Your SAP Connection
steps.description:
ABAP FS connects directly to your SAP system — no Eclipse needed.

> **Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P** opens the **Command Palette** — VS Code's search bar for all commands. You'll use it everywhere.

Press **Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P** → select **ABAP FS: Connection Manager** to add your first system.

Password is requested on first connect, not here.

[Open Connection Manager](command:abapfs.connectionManager)

steps.title: Connect to Your System
steps.description:
Press **Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P** → select **ABAP FS: Connect to an ABAP system**. Pick a system if you have more than one.

Password is prompted on first connection and stored securely. Once connected, SAP packages appear in the Explorer. Multiple systems appear simultaneously.

[Connect to SAP](command:abapfs.connect)

steps.title: Search for ABAP Objects
steps.description:
Press **Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P** → select **ABAP FS: ABAP Search for object** (shortcut: **Ctrl+Alt+;**).

First time: pick object types to search. Then type 2+ characters with wildcards (e.g. `Z*ORDER*`, `CL_BCS*`).

[Search for an Object](command:abapfs.search)

steps.title: Navigate Your Code
steps.description: ABAP objects open with full syntax highlighting. Use **Ctrl+Click** (Go to Definition), **Alt+F12** (Peek), **Shift+F12** (References). Hover over any symbol for rich metadata.

steps.title: Activate Your Code
steps.description:
Press **Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P** → select **Activate current object** (shortcut: **Alt+Shift+F3**), or click the **Activate icon** in the toolbar.

Saves first, prompts for transport if needed, shows related inactive objects to activate together.

steps.title: Data Browser
steps.description:
Open a **table, view, or CDS view** in the editor, then click the **database icon** in the toolbar.

Two modes: **Search mode** (field filters, operators, column selection) and **SQL Mode** (raw SELECT). Read-only.

steps.title: Run a Transaction
steps.description:
Press **Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P** → select **ABAP FS: Run SAP Transaction**. Type a transaction code (3+ characters). No file needs to be open.

Default: opens in embedded WebGUI inside VS Code. Configurable per system.

[Run a Transaction](command:abapfs.runTransaction)

steps.title: SAP GUI Toolbar Buttons
steps.description:
Three SAP GUI buttons appear in the editor toolbar when an ABAP file is open:

- **Play circle** — Embedded WebGUI (**Ctrl+Shift+F7**)
- **Desktop** — Native SAP GUI (**Ctrl+Shift+F5**)
- **Globe** — Browser WebGUI (**Ctrl+Shift+F6**)

Opens the current object in SAP GUI.

walkthroughs.title: ABAP FS Views & Tools
walkthroughs.description: Transports, debugging, code quality, dumps, traces, and version history — all inside VS Code.

steps.title: Already Know the Views? Skip Ahead
steps.description:
Already familiar with the views and tools? Jump ahead:

[AI Tools for ABAP](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%22murbani.vscode-abap-remote-fs%23abapfs.aiTools%22) — Use Copilot or any AI client with your SAP system.

[Advanced & Power Features](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%22murbani.vscode-abap-remote-fs%23abapfs.advanced%22) — MCP server, debug recording, subagents, heartbeat.

New here? Start with [Getting Started](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%22murbani.vscode-abap-remote-fs%23abapfs.gettingStarted%22).

Reopen anytime: **Help** menu → **Welcome**.

steps.title: Transport Management
steps.description:
Click the **Abap FS** icon in the Activity Bar (far left). Expand the **Transports** view to see your open transport requests.

Release, diff, copy numbers, run ATC on transports. Transports are selected/created when saving to non-local packages.

[Open Transports](command:abapfs.refreshtransports)

steps.title: ABAP Debugging
steps.description:
1. Open an ABAP object, set breakpoints (click gutter)
2. **Ctrl+Shift+D** → select **ABAP on server** → pick system if multiple → **F5** to attach
3. Trigger your program from SAP GUI, embedded GUI, or another session

steps.title: ATC — Code Quality Checks
steps.description:
With an ABAP object open, press **Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P** → select **ABAP FS: Run ABAP Test cockpit** (shortcut: **Ctrl+Shift+F2**).

Results: inline squiggles + **ATC Finds** view in the Abap FS panel.

steps.title: ABAP Unit Tests
steps.description:
With a class or program open, press **Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P** → select **ABAP FS: Run ABAP Unit Tests** (shortcut: **Ctrl+Shift+F11**).

Results in VS Code's Testing panel.

steps.title: ABAP Cleaner — Code Formatting
steps.description:
**First time:** **Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P** → select **ABAP FS: Setup ABAP Cleaner Integration**. Browse for `abap-cleanerc.exe` (note the **c**) or download from GitHub.

**After setup:** Click the **ruler icon** in the toolbar, or shortcut **Ctrl+Shift+Alt+F**.

[Set Up ABAP Cleaner](command:abapfs.setupCleaner)

steps.title: Runtime Error Dumps
steps.description: Open the **Abap FS** panel → expand **Dumps** view. Click any dump for full details. Like **ST22** inside VS Code.

steps.title: Performance Traces
steps.description: Open the **Abap FS** panel → expand **Traces** view. Create trace configurations, start/stop runs, view execution statistics. Like **SAT/SE30** inside VS Code.

steps.title: Blame Gutter & Version History
steps.description:
Click the **eye icon** in the editor toolbar to toggle blame on/off (or **Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P** → **ABAP FS: Show Blame**, shortcut **Ctrl+Alt+B**).

Choose between the original inline layout and a GitLens-inspired fixed blame lane with the **ABAP FS: Blame Render Mode** setting. Hover annotations still show author, date, and transport. Revision CodeLens above objects remains available for version comparison.

walkthroughs.title: AI Tools for ABAP
walkthroughs.description: Use AI to search, read, query, analyze, and visualize your SAP system — with GitHub Copilot or any MCP-compatible AI client.

steps.title: Already Using AI Tools? Skip Ahead
steps.description:
Already familiar with the AI tools? Jump ahead:

[Advanced & Power Features](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%22murbani.vscode-abap-remote-fs%23abapfs.advanced%22) — MCP server, debug recording, subagents, heartbeat.

Or go back to [Getting Started](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%22murbani.vscode-abap-remote-fs%23abapfs.gettingStarted%22) or [Views & Tools](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%22murbani.vscode-abap-remote-fs%23abapfs.viewsAndTools%22).

Reopen anytime: **Help** menu → **Welcome**.

steps.title: Set Up AI Access
steps.description:
**Copilot:** Click the Copilot icon in the sidebar → switch to **Agent** mode → ask a question. Allow tool calls once, per session, or always.

**Other AI clients:** See MCP Server in the Advanced walkthrough.

**Multiple systems:** Tell the AI which system. Works across systems in one chat.

[Open Copilot Chat](command:workbench.action.chat.open)

steps.title: Search & Read Objects
steps.description:
Ask the AI to find and read ABAP objects. It searches, reads source code, and explains in plain language.

[Try it: Find report RBDMANI2 and explain what it does](command:abapfs.openChatWithQuery?%22Find%20report%20RBDMANI2%20and%20explain%20what%20it%20does%22)

steps.title: Where-Used Analysis
steps.description:
Ask the AI to find all callers of a function, method, or object.

[Try it: Where is IDOC_INBOUND_ASYNCHRONOUS called from?](command:abapfs.openChatWithQuery?%22Where%20is%20function%20module%20IDOC_INBOUND_ASYNCHRONOUS%20called%20from%3F%22)

steps.title: Version History via AI
steps.description:
Ask the AI to show version history, diffs, and who changed what. Use your own custom objects (Z*/Y*) for best results.

[Try it: Show version history](command:abapfs.openChatWithQuery?%22Show%20the%20version%20history%20for%20report%20RBDAPP01%22)

steps.title: Query SAP Tables
steps.description:
Ask the AI to query SAP tables. Two modes: **internal** (text results for analysis) and **UI** (interactive grid).

[Try it: Show last 10 entries in EDIDC](command:abapfs.openChatWithQuery?%22Show%20me%20the%20last%2010%20entries%20in%20table%20EDIDC%20sorted%20by%20CREDAT%20descending%22)

steps.title: Run ATC via AI
steps.description:
Ask the AI to run ATC checks and explain findings. Use your own custom objects (Z*/Y*) for meaningful results.

[Try it: Run ATC on RBDAPP01](command:abapfs.openChatWithQuery?%22Run%20ATC%20on%20report%20RBDAPP01%20and%20explain%20the%20findings%22)

steps.title: Run Unit Tests via AI
steps.description:
Ask the AI to run ABAP Unit tests and explain results.

[Try it: Run unit tests for CL_ABAP_UNIT_ASSERT](command:abapfs.openChatWithQuery?%22Run%20unit%20tests%20for%20CL_ABAP_UNIT_ASSERT%20and%20show%20results%22)

steps.title: View Transports via AI
steps.description:
Ask the AI to list and inspect your transports. It can view and compare, but cannot release or modify.

[Try it: Show my open transport requests](command:abapfs.openChatWithQuery?%22Show%20my%20open%20transport%20requests%22)

steps.title: Dump & Trace Analysis
steps.description:
Ask the AI to analyze dumps (ST22 data) and performance traces. It explains root causes and suggests fixes.

[Try it: Analyze recent dumps](command:abapfs.openChatWithQuery?%22Analyze%20the%20most%20recent%20dumps%20in%20this%20system%22)

steps.title: Generate Diagrams from Code
steps.description:
Ask the AI to generate Mermaid diagrams from your code — class, sequence, ER, flowchart, and more.

[Try it: Create a class diagram for CL_ABAP_TYPEDESCR](command:abapfs.openChatWithQuery?%22Create%20a%20class%20diagram%20showing%20CL_ABAP_TYPEDESCR%20and%20its%20subclasses%22)

steps.title: AI Skills — Built-in Expertise
steps.description: 7 built-in AI skills for ABAP development. Type **/** in Copilot Chat to see them, or ask a relevant question — skills activate automatically.

steps.title: Ask AI About ABAP FS Itself
steps.description:
The AI has access to full ABAP FS documentation and settings. Ask it anything about how ABAP FS works.

[Ask about ABAP FS features](command:abapfs.openChatWithQuery?%22What%20features%20does%20ABAP%20FS%20have%3F%20Give%20me%20an%20overview%22)

walkthroughs.title: Advanced & Power Features
walkthroughs.description: MCP server, debug recording, dependency graphs, feeds, cross-system comparison, subagents, and heartbeat.

steps.title: Other Walkthroughs
steps.description:
Looking for something else?

[Getting Started with ABAP FS](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%22murbani.vscode-abap-remote-fs%23abapfs.gettingStarted%22) — Connect, search, edit, activate, browse data.

[ABAP FS Views & Tools](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%22murbani.vscode-abap-remote-fs%23abapfs.viewsAndTools%22) — Transports, debugging, ATC, unit tests, dumps, traces.

[AI Tools for ABAP](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%22murbani.vscode-abap-remote-fs%23abapfs.aiTools%22) — Use Copilot or any AI client with your SAP system.

Reopen anytime: **Help** menu → **Welcome**.

steps.title: MCP Server for External AI Clients
steps.description:
**Not using Copilot?** Enable the built-in MCP server to connect Cursor, Claude, Windsurf, etc. Same 39 tools.

Workspace Settings → `abapfs.mcpServer.autoStart` → port `4847`. Set API key for security.

steps.title: Record & Replay Debug Sessions
steps.description:
Record a debug session, replay offline — even without SAP. Share `.abaprecord` files with teammates.

**Record:** While debugging → **Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P** → **ABAP FS: Start Debug Recording**
**Replay:** **Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P** → **ABAP FS: Replay Debug Recording**

[Replay a Recording](command:abapfs.replaySession)

steps.title: Dependency Graph
steps.description:
Open an ABAP object → right-click → **Visualize Dependency Graph**. Shows a where-used graph.

Cursor on a symbol → who uses that symbol. Cursor on empty space → who references the entire object.

steps.title: Feed Inbox & Notifications
steps.description:
**Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P** → select **ABAP FS: Configure Feeds**. Pick feeds per system. View entries in the **Feed Inbox** (Abap FS panel).

[Configure Feeds](command:abapfs.configureFeeds)

steps.title: Text Elements Manager
steps.description:
With an ABAP program/class open, **Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P** → select **ABAP FS: Text Elements Manager**, or right-click → **Manage Text Elements**.

Edit selection texts, text symbols, message class texts. Falls back to SAP GUI on older systems.

steps.title: Cross-System Comparison
steps.description:
With an ABAP object open, right-click → **Compare With Other System**, or **Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P** → select **ABAP FS: ABAP Compare With another SAP System**.

Compare across any connected systems. Requires at least two.

steps.title: ADT Communication Log
steps.description:
**Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P** → select **ABAP FS: Activate ADT Communication Log**. Pick a system if multiple connected. Logs every HTTP request/response.

[Activate Comm Log](command:abapfs.activateCommLog)

steps.title: AI Subagents
steps.description:
Delegate tasks to cheaper AI models. Ask Copilot: *"Set up ABAP subagents"* — it configures models for all 13 agents, then enables them.

> Cannot enable until all agents have models assigned.

steps.title: Heartbeat Monitoring
steps.description:
Autonomous AI agent running tasks on a schedule. Ask Copilot: *"Set up heartbeat monitoring"* — it configures a model and adds tasks.

Categories: transport, dump, job, idoc, performance, reminder, custom.

[Open Heartbeat Config](command:abapfs.openHeartbeatJson)
